commit.name: Weather Card
commit.description: Data display with props, design tokens, and responsive layout
commit.prompt:
Build a weather card component. It should display:
- Current temperature (large, prominent)
- Weather condition with an appropriate icon/emoji (sunny, cloudy, rainy, etc.)
- Humidity percentage
- Wind speed
- A 5-day forecast strip at the bottom showing day name, icon, and high/low temps

All data must come from props — never hardcode weather data. Use CSS variables from the design system for all colors and spacing.

issues.description: Forecast strip is not constrained to 5 items; it renders whatever length is provided, which can violate the explicit 5-day requirement in the UI.
issues.fix: Limit rendering to first 5 forecast entries (e.g., forecast.slice(0, 5)) and optionally handle fewer than 5 with placeholders.

issues.description: Current condition icon is derived from condition text, but forecast icons are taken directly from props without fallback mapping from condition-like values.
issues.fix: If forecast icon field can contain condition text, normalize via the same icon-mapping helper or clarify prop contract to require emoji/icon token.

issues.description: Forecast cards define transition styles but no corresponding hover/focus state changes, so interaction polish is incomplete.
issues.fix: Add :hover/:focus-visible style changes (e.g., subtle lift/background/border emphasis) and ensure keyboard focus indication.

issues.description: No explicit accessible labeling for emoji-based weather icons; screen readers may announce ambiguous characters.
issues.fix: Add aria-label/title for icons or visually hidden text describing condition per icon.

issues.description: Forecast list uses role=list/listitem on generic containers, but semantic list elements would be more robust.
issues.fix: Use semantic ul/li (or equivalent primitives) where possible for forecast items.

issues.description: Optional numeric forecast fields default to 0, which can present misleading weather data when missing.
issues.fix: Use null/placeholder display (e.g., '—') for absent high/low values instead of 0.

evaluations.critique: The component is well-structured and visually polished, with clear hierarchy, strong temperature emphasis, and good use of card sections for current metrics plus forecast. It largely satisfies the prompt and uses props-driven data appropriately, but interaction polish is limited because hover/focus states are not actually implemented despite transition declarations. Accessibility can be improved by adding descriptive labels for emoji icons and stronger semantic list markup.

evaluation.critique: The component has solid layout and design token usage with proper CSS variables throughout, but falls short on polish with missing hover/focus states on forecast items and a hardcoded placeholder text ('Feels calm...') that contradicts the data-driven requirement. The truncated code at the end also suggests incomplete implementation of the forecast card rendering.

commit.name: Multi-Step Survey Form
commit.description: Multi-step form with validation, progress tracking, and review
commit.prompt:
Build a multi-step survey form with 4 steps:
- Step 1: Text inputs for name (required) and email (required, must be valid email format) with inline validation
- Step 2: Radio button group for satisfaction rating (1-5 scale with labels from props)
- Step 3: Checkbox group for feature interests (options from props)
- Step 4: Textarea for open-ended comments (optional, with character counter, max 500 chars)

Requirements:
- Progress bar at the top showing current step (e.g., "Step 2 of 4")
- Back/Next navigation buttons (Next disabled until required fields valid)
- Final review step showing all answers before submission
- Call props.onSubmit with all collected answers as a structured object
- All option labels and feature lists must come from props
- Use design system CSS variables for all styling

featureOptions.description: Available feature options for the checkbox step

satisfactionLabels.description: Labels for satisfaction rating (e.g., Very Unsatisfied to Very Satisfied)

submit.label: Submit Survey
submit.description: Called with all collected answers as a structured object

commit.name: Kanban Task Board
commit.description: Column-based task board with task cards and state management
commit.prompt:
Build a Kanban-style task board with 3 columns: To Do, In Progress, Done.

Each column:
- Has a header with column name and task count
- Contains a vertical list of task cards
- Has an "Add Task" button at the bottom

Each task card displays:
- Task title (editable on double-click or via edit button)
- Priority badge (low/medium/high with color coding from design tokens)
- Assignee avatar or initials
- Due date (if set)
- A move button or dropdown to move the task to another column

Requirements:
- Tasks can be moved between columns via move controls (buttons or select dropdown)
- New tasks start in "To Do" column
- "Add Task" shows an inline form with title input and priority selector
- Track total tasks and completed count in a summary bar at the top
- Initial tasks come from props; real-time updates from other team members arrive via stream
- Call the taskUpdate action when a task is moved, edited, or created
- Use design system CSS variables for all colors, spacing, and shadows

tasks.description: Array of tasks with id, title, priority (low/medium/high), assignee initials, column ID, and optional dueDate

columns.description: Column definitions: [{id: "todo", name: "To Do"}, {id: "in-progress", name: "In Progress"}, {id: "done", name: "Done"}]

taskUpdate.label: Task Updated
taskUpdate.description: Called when a task is moved, edited, or created. Payload: { action, taskId, data }

stream.description: Real-time task updates from other team members

taskChanged.description: A task was moved, edited, or created by another user — update it in the board

getBoardState.description: Current board state including any unsaved edits

commit.name: Interactive Periodic Table
commit.description: CSS Grid periodic table with category coloring, search, and detail panel
commit.prompt:
Build an interactive periodic table of elements.

Layout:
- CSS Grid: 18 columns. Place each element using gridRow and gridColumn from its data.
- Each cell: symbol (large, centered), atomic number (small, top-left)
- Color-code by category using design tokens (e.g., noble-gas → one color, halogen → another)
- Search input at top — filters by name or symbol (case-insensitive)
- Click an element → detail panel below grid shows: name, symbol, atomicNumber, atomicWeight, category
- Legend showing each category with its color

All element data comes from props.elements. Use design system CSS variables.

issues.description: Source code is truncated mid-style declaration (`left: '`), so component is incomplete and remaining required UI parts (detail panel and legend rendering) are not verifiable/present in provided snippet.
issues.fix: Provide complete component code and ensure full render path includes element cells, detail panel fields (name, symbol, atomicNumber, atomicWeight, category), and category legend.

issues.description: Selected element logic defaults to first filtered element and mutates selection via effects, which may not match expected click-driven detail behavior when search changes.
issues.fix: Keep explicit selected element state; on filter changes, clear selection or preserve if still visible, without forcing implicit reselection unless specified.

issues.description: Category color mapping reuses same palettes for multiple categories (e.g., halogen/alkaline-earth, noble-gas/transition-metal), weakening legend differentiation.
issues.fix: Assign clearly distinct token-based colors per category for stronger visual separation.

issues.description: Hover/focus effects are implemented via direct DOM style mutation in event handlers, which is brittle and can conflict with rerenders.
issues.fix: Use component state/CSS pseudo-states or design-system props for hover/focus/active styles instead of imperative mutations.

issues.description: Grid cells are focusable `Box` elements with keyboard handlers but lack semantic button role; interactive non-button elements can reduce assistive tech clarity.
issues.fix: Render interactive cells as semantic buttons (or add `role="button"` plus proper key handling and aria-pressed/selected semantics).

issues.description: Redundant effects synchronize `selectedAtomicNumber` and `selectedElement`, creating unnecessary state churn and potential render loops.
issues.fix: Derive `selectedElement` from a single source of truth and remove circular synchronization effects.

evaluations.critique: The component shows solid intent: 18-column grid placement, searchable dataset, and keyboard-click selection patterns are present, with decent spacing and transitions. However, the provided code is incomplete/truncated, preventing confirmation of required detail panel and legend output, and there are structural concerns around state synchronization and interactive semantics. Finish the implementation and simplify selection logic while improving category differentiation and semantic accessibility to reach production quality.

issues.description: The provided source is truncated and does not show completion of the element cell content (symbol), detail panel rendering, or legend UI, so key requested features cannot be verified as implemented.
issues.fix: Ensure full component includes: symbol centered in each cell, detail panel below grid showing all required fields, and a category-color legend.

issues.description: Selection is tied to `filteredElements.find(...)`, so selected element details disappear when search excludes it; this may conflict with expected click-to-view detail behavior.
issues.fix: Resolve selected element from the full `elements` array (or keep last selected object in state) while separately controlling grid filtering.

issues.description: Hover/focus states are implemented via inline event handlers that mutate DOM styles directly, which is less robust and can cause inconsistencies.
issues.fix: Use declarative style/state-driven variants (or CSS pseudo-states) for hover/focus/active transitions instead of imperative mutation.

issues.description: Gridcells are made focusable and clickable directly, but semantic button behavior is not guaranteed; screen reader interaction patterns for actionable cells may be suboptimal.
issues.fix: Render an actual button inside each gridcell (or use button semantics on the interactive element) with clear accessible name and focus ring.

issues.description: `selectedAtomicNumber` can become `null` for elements missing atomic numbers, creating ambiguous selection and potential unstable behavior.
issues.fix: Use a stable unique key for selection (e.g., atomicNumber required, or fallback composite id), and avoid selecting items with missing identifiers.

evaluations.critique: The component demonstrates a strong base: clean sorting/filtering logic, 18-column grid placement, and polished card-based structure with transitions. However, the snippet is incomplete, so core requirements like the full cell content, detail panel, and legend cannot be fully confirmed. Accessibility and maintainability would improve by using semantic button elements and declarative interaction styling.

evaluation.critique: The component uses CSS Grid correctly for the periodic table layout with proper 18-column structure and responsive overflow handling. Design tokens are mostly used appropriately, though some fallback hex values are present which slightly detracts from consistency. The main issues are: incomplete source code (truncated mid-style), missing legend implementation despite being mentioned in requirements, and limited visual feedback for interactive states (hover effects not fully visible in the snippet).

commit.name: E-Commerce Product Page
commit.description: Product detail page with image, tabs, quantity selector, and cart
commit.prompt:
Build an e-commerce product detail page. Layout:

Top section:
- Product image placeholder (use a colored box with the product name)
- Product title, price (with currency from props), and rating stars (1-5)
- Stock status badge ("In Stock" green or "Low Stock" warning)

Middle section — Tab navigation with 3 tabs:
- "Description" tab: renders product description text from props
- "Specifications" tab: renders key-value pairs from props as a table
- "Reviews" tab: renders a list of reviews (author, rating, text, date) from props

Bottom section:
- Quantity selector (increment/decrement buttons, min 1, max from stock)
- "Add to Cart" button (disabled if out of stock)
- Call props.onAddToCart with { productId, quantity } when clicked

Requirements:
- All product data from props — nothing hardcoded
- Use design system tokens for all colors, spacing, typography
- Price should format with currency symbol from props
- Rating stars should be visual (filled/empty)
- Responsive: stack image above details on mobile widths

product.description: Product object with id, name, price, currency, rating, stock, description, specifications[], reviews[]

commit.prompt:
Build a chat interface component with real-time messaging. Layout:

Header:
- Chat title from props
- Participant count badge
- Typing indicator (shows when another user is typing, received via stream)

Message list:

commit.name: Stock Ticker Dashboard
commit.description: Stock price display with color-coded changes and responsive grid
commit.prompt:
Build a stock ticker dashboard that displays stock prices with live updates. Layout:
- Header with "Market Watch" title and a "Last Updated" timestamp (updates in real-time)
- Responsive grid of stock cards (3 per row on desktop, 1 on mobile)
- Each card shows: ticker symbol (large, bold), company name, current price formatted with 2 decimals, change amount, and change percentage
- Color coding: green text/border for positive change, red for negative change
- A subtle arrow icon (▲ or ▼) next to the change percentage
- Brief flash/highlight animation when a stock price updates

Initial stock data comes from props. Live price updates arrive via stream events — merge them into the displayed data. Use design system CSS variables for all colors and spacing.

commit.name: Onboarding Wizard
commit.description: Multi-step onboarding flow with step indicator and validation
commit.prompt:
Build a 3-step onboarding wizard. Steps:

Step 1 - Profile Setup:
- Name input (required)
- Email input (required, validate email format)
- Avatar selection (choose from 4 emoji avatars)

Step 2 - Preferences:
- Role selection (dropdown: Developer, Designer, Manager, Other)
- Notification toggle (email notifications on/off)
- Theme preference (light/dark toggle)

Step 3 - Review & Confirm:
- Summary of all selections from steps 1-2
- "Go Back" button to edit
- "Complete Setup" button to submit

Requirements:
- Step indicator at top showing current step (1/2/3) with progress bar
- Back/Next navigation (Next disabled until required fields valid)
- Smooth transition between steps
- Call props.onComplete with all collected data on final submit
- All initial values from props (for editing existing profile)
- Use design system CSS variables for all styling

initialProfile.description: Pre-filled profile data for editing (all fields optional)

avatarOptions.description: Available avatar emojis to choose from

roleOptions.description: Available role options for the dropdown

complete.label: Complete Setup
complete.description: Called with all profile data when user completes onboarding
